## Comprehensive Ratatui 0.30 + Crossterm 0.29 TUI Improvements Guide

Based on my research, here's what's available and recommended for your ratatui 0.30 TUI application:

---

## 1. Scrollable Viewports & Scroll State Management

### Built-in Ratatui Approach (Recommended for Simple Cases)

**`ScrollbarState` (Native to Ratatui 0.30)**
- Core constructor: `ScrollbarState::new(content_length: usize)`
- Fluent builder methods: `.position(usize)`, `.content_length(usize)`, `.viewport_content_length(usize)`
- Navigation: `.next()`, `.prev()`, `.first()`, `.last()`, `.scroll(ScrollDirection)`, `.get_position()`
- **Critical detail**: Must set `content_length` explicitly or Scrollbar won't render (default is 0)
- Derives `Clone`, `Copy`, `Debug`, `Default` — suitable for persistent state across render cycles
- **Implementation pattern**: Maintain scroll state in your app state struct, update on events, pass to Paragraph's `.scroll((y, x))` method

**Reference Documentation**:
- [ScrollbarState - docs.rs](https://docs.rs/ratatui/latest/ratatui/widgets/struct.ScrollbarState.html)
- [Scrollbar widget - docs.rs](https://docs.rs/ratatui/latest/ratatui/widgets/struct.Scrollbar.html)

### External Widget: tui-scrollview

- **Status**: Archived in September 2024; functionality consolidated into broader **tui-widgets** ecosystem
- **Features**: `ScrollView` widget + `ScrollViewState` for managing scroll position
- **Current recommendation**: Use native Ratatui `ScrollbarState` instead, as tui-scrollview is legacy
- Reference: [tui-scrollview GitHub](https://github.com/joshka/tui-scrollview) (archived)

---

## 2. Text Wrapping in Paragraph Widgets

**Native Ratatui Support**:
- Use `Paragraph::wrap(Wrap { trim: true })` to enable text wrapping
- Trimming whitespace automatically during wrapping
- Combine with `.scroll((y, x))` offset for controlled viewport
- **Note**: Wrapping is currently only supported in the Paragraph widget

**Implementation Example**:
```rust
Paragraph::new("Your text here")
    .wrap(Wrap { trim: true })
    .scroll((scroll_offset_y, scroll_offset_x))
```

**Reference**: [Paragraph widget docs - Ratatui](https://docs.rs/ratatui/latest/ratatui/widgets/struct.Paragraph.html) | [Paragraph example](https://ratatui.rs/examples/widgets/paragraph/)

---

## 3. Keyboard-Driven Scrolling (Page Up/Down, Arrow Keys)

### Page Up/Down and Keyboard Control

**Pattern for Paragraph + Scrollbar**:
1. Maintain scroll state in your app: `scroll_offset: (u16, u16)`
2. Handle keyboard events via crossterm (ratatui doesn't provide input handling)
3. Update scroll position on `KeyCode::PageUp`/`KeyCode::PageDown`
4. Pass to Paragraph: `.scroll(scroll_offset)`

**For tui-textarea (Multi-line Input)**:
- Use `Scrolling::PageDown` / `Scrolling::PageUp` methods
- Built-in keyboard navigation with Emacs-like bindings (C-n/C-p/C-f/C-b)
- Auto-scrolls cursor into view

**Reference**: [Ratatui scrollbar example](https://ratatui.rs/examples/widgets/scrollbar/) | [Scrolling in ratatui-textarea docs](https://docs.rs/ratatui-textarea/latest/ratatui_textarea/enum.Scrolling.html)

### Mouse Wheel Scrolling

**Crossterm Integration**:
1. Enable mouse capture: `ExecuteOnce::new(EnableMouseCapture::new()).execute()?`
2. Match on `Event::Mouse(MouseEvent { kind: MouseEventKind::ScrollUp/ScrollDown, .. })`
3. Update scroll state and re-render

**Reference**: [Mouse Capture - Ratatui docs](https://ratatui.rs/concepts/backends/mouse-capture/) | [Event Handling - Ratatui docs](https://ratatui.rs/concepts/event-handling/)

---

## 4. Text Input Widgets: tui-textarea

### Overview

**tui-textarea** is a feature-rich, well-maintained multi-line text editor widget for ratatui.

### Version Compatibility

- **Latest**: v0.7.0 (actively maintained)
- **Ratatui support**: `>=0.23.0, <1` (broad compatibility)
- **tui-textarea v0.7.0 works with ratatui 0.30 ✓**
- **Crossterm**: Shared with ratatui (0.29 compatible via ratatui's transitive dep)
- **Important**: Ensure all dependencies use matching versions of crossterm/termion

### Key Features

- Multi-line and single-line modes
- Emacs-like keyboard shortcuts (C-n/C-p/C-f/C-b, M-f/M-b for word nav)
- Undo/Redo functionality
- Line numbers and cursor line highlighting
- Regular expression search (optional feature)
- Text selection and mouse scrolling support
- Yank buffer for deleted text operations
- Backend-agnostic (crossterm, termion, termwiz)
- Multiple textarea instances on same screen

### Integration Pattern

```rust
use tui_textarea::TextArea;

let mut textarea = TextArea::default();
textarea.insert_char('a');
frame.render_widget(&textarea, area);
```

**References**:
- [tui-textarea GitHub](https://github.com/rhysd/tui-textarea)
- [tui-textarea Docs.rs](https://docs.rs/crate/tui-textarea/latest)
- [tui-textarea Examples](https://github.com/rhysd/tui-textarea/blob/main/examples/single_line.rs)
- [Ratatui recommendations on input](https://ratatui.rs/faq/)

---

## 5. Markdown Rendering in Ratatui

### tui-markdown

**Status**: Experimental Proof of Concept (active as of Dec 2025)

**What it does**:
- Parses Markdown using `pulldown-cmark`
- Converts to `ratatui::Text` for display in Paragraph widgets
- Primary API: `from_str(markdown_str) -> ratatui::Text`

**Features**:
- Syntax highlighting (optional `highlight-code` feature, enabled by default)
- Converts common Markdown elements (headers, bold, italics, code blocks, etc.)
- Used by 164+ dependent crates

**Related Project**:
- **markdown-reader**: Standalone TUI app that uses tui-markdown to render .md files
- Latest release: v0.1.27 (Dec 2025)

**Limitations**:
- Experimental/PoC status — API may change
- Limited to Text rendering (not interactive)
- No live preview or editing features

**Usage Pattern**:
```rust
use tui_markdown::from_str;

let text = from_str("# Hello\nThis is **bold**");
frame.render_widget(Paragraph::new(text), area);
```

**References**:
- [tui-markdown GitHub](https://github.com/joshka/tui-markdown)
- [tui-markdown Docs.rs](https://docs.rs/tui-markdown)
- [markdown-reader crates.io](https://crates.io/crates/markdown-reader)
- [markdown-reader GitHub](https://github.com/joshka/tui-markdown/tree/main/markdown-reader)

---

## 6. Common TUI Patterns

### Command History (Up/Down Arrow Recall)

**Implementation Pattern**:
1. Maintain a `Vec<String>` of command history in app state
2. Track current history position with `u16 history_index`
3. On Up arrow: `history_index -= 1` and populate input from `history[index]`
4. On Down arrow: `history_index += 1` (or clear input if at end)
5. Use `tui-textarea` for the input widget (has built-in history-like navigation)

**Note**: tui-textarea supports Emacs-style navigation (C-p/C-n) for movement; you'll need custom handling for arrow key command recall.

### Multi-line Input Editing

**Use tui-textarea**:
- Full multi-line support with cursor tracking
- Auto-scrolls to keep cursor visible
- Word navigation (M-f, M-b)
- Line operations (C-k to delete line, C-u to delete before cursor)
- Yank buffer for clipboard-like behavior

**References**:
- [tui-textarea multi-line support](https://github.com/rhysd/tui-textarea#features)
- [ratatui-textarea variant](https://docs.rs/ratatui-textarea/latest/ratatui_textarea/) (alternative fork)

### Status Bar / Footer Bar Pattern

**Layout Approach** (Native Ratatui):
```rust
let chunks = Layout::vertical([
    Constraint::Length(1),  // Header
    Constraint::Fill(1),    // Main content
    Constraint::Length(1),  // Status/Footer
]).split(area);

frame.render_widget(header_widget, chunks[0]);
frame.render_widget(content_widget, chunks[1]);
frame.render_widget(status_widget, chunks[2]);
```

**Status Widget Implementation**:
- Use `Paragraph::new(status_text).centered()` for footer
- Use `Block::default().borders(Borders::TOP)` for separator
- Update status dynamically based on app state

**References**:
- [Layout constraints example - Ratatui](https://ratatui.rs/examples/layout/constraints/)
- [List example with footer - Ratatui](https://ratatui.rs/examples/widgets/list/)

### Focus Management Between Panes

**Native Pattern**:
1. Enum for focus state: `enum Focus { InputPane, ListView, ... }`
2. Store in app state
3. Route events based on focus: `match self.focus { Focus::InputPane => { ... } }`
4. Highlight focused pane with `.block(Block::default().border_style(Style::new().fg(Color::Yellow)))`

**Third-party Solutions**:
- **tui-window**: Minimal focus manager for page-based apps (loose HTML-like semantics)
- **rat-salsa**: Event queue with focus handling and dialog windows
- **rat-widget**: Structural widgets (split/tabbed layouts) with built-in focus
- **tui-realm**: Full framework with component reusability (React/Elm-inspired)

**Recommendation**: For simple 2-3 pane layouts, native enum-based focus is sufficient and doesn't add dependencies.

**References**:
- [tui-window GitHub](https://github.com/untitled-/tuiwindow)
- [tui-realm GitHub](https://github.com/veeso/tui-realm)
- [Awesome Ratatui - Focus Management](https://github.com/ratatui/awesome-ratatui)

### Popup / Modal Dialogs

**Native Ratatui Pattern** (Recommended):
```rust
// 1. Render background first
frame.render_widget(main_content, full_area);

// 2. Calculate centered popup area
let popup_area = centered_rect(60, 40, full_area);

// 3. Clear the area
frame.render_widget(Clear, popup_area);

// 4. Render popup on top
frame.render_widget(popup_widget, popup_area);

// Helper for centering
fn centered_rect(percent_x: u16, percent_y: u16, r: Rect) -> Rect {
    let layout = Layout::vertical([Constraint::Percentage((100 - percent_y) / 2), ...])
        .flex(Flex::Center)
        .split(r);
    // Calculate and return centered rect
}
```

**Key Widget**: `Clear` widget erases background before rendering popup

**Third-party**:
- **tui-popup**: Dedicated popup widget with centered rendering
- **tui-confirm-dialog**: Simple yes/no confirmations
- **tui-file-dialog**: File picker dialogs

**References**:
- [Popup example - Ratatui](https://ratatui.rs/examples/apps/popup/)
- [Clear widget - Ratatui docs](https://docs.rs/ratatui/latest/ratatui/widgets/struct.Clear.html)
- [tui-popup GitHub](https://github.com/joshka/tui-popup)

---

## 7. Code Syntax Highlighting

### syntect Integration

**Options**:

1. **syntect-tui** (Recommended for Ratatui):
   - Lightweight translation layer between syntect and ratatui style types
   - Converts highlighted code to `ratatui::text::Line`
   - Add to Cargo.toml: `syntect-tui = "3.0"`

2. **tui-syntax-highlight**:
   - Ratatui-specific highlighter
   - Returns `ratatui::Text` from code strings

3. **edtui** (Full editor widget):
   - Vim-inspired editor with syntect integration
   - Supports multiple Sublime Text themes

**Basic Pattern** (syntect-tui):
```rust
use syntect_tui::HighlightLines;

let highlighted = HighlightLines::new(language, theme)
    .highlight("your code here", ss);
// Convert to ratatui::Text for rendering
```

**References**:
- [syntect GitHub](https://github.com/trishume/syntect)
- [syntect-tui crates.io](https://lib.rs/crates/syntect-tui)
- [tui-syntax-highlight GitHub](https://github.com/aschey/tui-syntax-highlight)
- [edtui Docs.rs](https://docs.rs/edtui)

---

## Summary & Recommended Stack

| Feature | Solution | Status | Notes |
|---------|----------|--------|-------|
| **Scrolling** | Native `ScrollbarState` | ✓ Stable | Built-in, no deps needed |
| **Text Wrapping** | Native `Paragraph.wrap()` | ✓ Stable | Part of Ratatui core |
| **Keyboard Scrolling** | Native events + custom handling | ✓ Flexible | Page Up/Down via crossterm |
| **Mouse Scrolling** | Crossterm `Event::Mouse` | ✓ Works | Enable mouse capture |
| **Multi-line Input** | **tui-textarea v0.7** | ✓ Compatible | Broad ratatui support |
| **Markdown Rendering** | **tui-markdown** | ⚠ PoC | Experimental but working |
| **Status/Footer Bars** | Native `Layout` | ✓ Stable | No special widget needed |
| **Focus Management** | Native enum-based | ✓ Simple | For 2-3 panes, no deps |
| **Popups/Modals** | Native `Clear` + Layout | ✓ Stable | Or use `tui-popup` crate |
| **Code Highlighting** | **syntect-tui** | ✓ Works | For syntax-highlighted blocks |

**Dependency-Light Starter Kit**:
```toml
ratatui = { version = "0.30", features = ["crossterm"] }
crossterm = "0.29"
tui-textarea = "0.7"
syntect-tui = "3.0"
```

---

## Sources

- [Ratatui Official Documentation](https://ratatui.rs/)
- [Ratatui Docs.rs Reference](https://docs.rs/ratatui/latest/ratatui/)
- [ScrollbarState - docs.rs](https://docs.rs/ratatui/latest/ratatui/widgets/struct.ScrollbarState.html)
- [Paragraph Widget - docs.rs](https://docs.rs/ratatui/latest/ratatui/widgets/struct.Paragraph.html)
- [tui-scrollview GitHub](https://github.com/joshka/tui-scrollview)
- [tui-textarea GitHub](https://github.com/rhysd/tui-textarea)
- [tui-textarea Docs.rs](https://docs.rs/crate/tui-textarea/latest)
- [tui-markdown GitHub](https://github.com/joshka/tui-markdown)
- [tui-markdown Docs.rs](https://docs.rs/tui-markdown)
- [Ratatui Event Handling Docs](https://ratatui.rs/concepts/event-handling/)
- [Ratatui Mouse Capture Docs](https://ratatui.rs/concepts/backends/mouse-capture/)
- [Scrollbar Example - Ratatui](https://ratatui.rs/examples/widgets/scrollbar/)
- [Popup Example - Ratatui](https://ratatui.rs/examples/apps/popup/)
- [Layout Constraints Example - Ratatui](https://ratatui.rs/examples/layout/constraints/)
- [syntect GitHub](https://github.com/trishume/syntect)
- [syntect-tui crates.io](https://lib.rs/crates/syntect-tui)
- [tui-window GitHub](https://github.com/untitled-/tuiwindow)
- [tui-realm GitHub](https://github.com/veeso/tui-realm)
- [tui-popup GitHub](https://github.com/joshka/tui-popup)
- [Awesome Ratatui - Community Resources](https://github.com/ratatui/awesome-ratatui)

input.prompt:
You are a Rust software architect designing a comprehensive TUI restructure for the Arcan agent runtime. Design a detailed implementation plan based on these requirements and constraints.

## Current State
- arcan-tui crate: 5 source files, ~1,488 LOC
- Stack: ratatui 0.30, crossterm 0.29, tokio, reqwest, reqwest-eventsource
- Files: lib.rs (entry), app.rs (event loop), models.rs (state), network.rs (HTTP/SSE), ui.rs (rendering)
- 15 unit tests across 3 files, 0 integration tests
- Working features: basic chat, SSE streaming, tool execution display, approval workflow
- Major gaps: no scroll, no markdown, no input history, no timestamps, no status bar, stubbed model switching

## User Requirements
1. **Primary goal**: Usability first — scroll, input history, status bar, markdown, error visibility
2. **Dependencies**: Rich — add tui-textarea 0.7, tui-markdown, syntect-tui 3.0
3. **Refactoring**: Full restructure — redesign module layout, widget abstraction layer, component-based architecture

## Available Daemon API (currently unused by TUI)
- GET /sessions — list sessions
- POST /sessions — create session
- GET /sessions/{id}/state — agent state vector, operating mode
- GET /sessions/{id}/events — event history (paginated)
- GET /sessions/{id}/branches — list branches
- POST /sessions/{id}/branches — create branch
- POST /sessions/{id}/branches/{id}/merge — merge branch

## Available External Crates
- tui-textarea 0.7: Multi-line input, undo/redo, Emacs keybindings, selection, works with ratatui 0.30
- tui-markdown: Converts markdown to ratatui::Text via pulldown-cmark (experimental but functional)
- syntect-tui 3.0: Code syntax highlighting for ratatui

## Design Constraints
- Rust 2024 Edition (edition = "2024", rust-version = "1.85")
- `gen` is reserved keyword — never use as identifier
- Error handling: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for binaries
- Must maintain all 15 existing tests passing
- Pure state model (AppState) must remain IO-free and testable
- Network layer already supports 3 SSE formats (protocol, Vercel v6, canonical)

## Design the following:

### 1. New Module Architecture
Propose a complete file/module tree with clear responsibilities. Consider:
- Widget abstraction layer (trait or enum-based)
- Separate files for each UI panel/widget
- Focus management system
- Theme/style configuration
- Event routing

### 2. Implementation Phases
Break into 4-5 phases, each independently shippable:
- Phase 1: Foundation (restructure + scroll + status bar)
- Phase 2: Rich input (tui-textarea + command history)
- Phase 3: Markdown + syntax highlighting
- Phase 4: Polish (error visibility, timestamps, tool results, loading indicators)
- Phase 5: Advanced features (session browser, state inspector — stretch)

### 3. For each phase, specify:
- Files to create/modify
- Key structs/traits/functions
- Dependencies added
- Tests to add
- Verification steps

### 4. Key Design Decisions
- How to handle focus between panes (chat log vs input)
- Scroll state management approach
- How markdown rendering integrates with UiBlock
- Command history persistence (in-memory vs file)
- Theme system design
- How to surface errors to users (inline vs popup vs status bar)

Provide a concrete, actionable plan with file paths relative to arcan/crates/arcan-tui/.

- GET /sessions/{id}/state — agent state vector, operating mode
- GET /sessions/{id}/events — event history (paginated)
- GET /sessions/{id}/branches — list branches
- POST /sessions/{id}/branches — create branch
- POST /sessions/{id}/branches/{id}/merge — merge branch

## Available External Crates
- tui-textarea 0.7: Multi-line input, undo/redo, Emacs keybindings, selection, works with ratatui 0.30
- tui-markdown: Converts markdown to ratatui::Text via pulldown-cmark (experimental but functional)
- syntect-tui 3.0: Code syntax highlighting for ratatui

## Design Constraints
- Rust 2024 Edition (edition = "2024", rust-version = "1.85")
- `gen` is reserved keyword — never use as identifier
- Error handling: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for binaries

- Pure state model (AppState) must remain IO-free and testable
- Network layer already supports 3 SSE formats (protocol, Vercel v6, canonical)

## Design the following:

### 1. New Module Architecture
Propose a complete file/module tree with clear responsibilities. Consider:
- Widget abstraction layer (trait or enum-based)
- Separate files for each UI panel/widget
- Focus management system
- Theme/style configuration
- Event routing

Break into 4-5 phases, each independently shippable:
- Phase 1: Foundation (restructure + scroll + status bar)
- Phase 2: Rich input (tui-textarea + command history)
- Phase 3: Markdown + syntax highlighting
- Phase 4: Polish (error visibility, timestamps, tool results, loading indicators)
- Phase 5: Advanced features (session browser, state inspector — stretch)

### 3. For each phase, specify:
- Files to create/modify
- Key structs/traits/functions
- Dependencies added
- Tests to add
- Verification steps

### 4. Key Design Decisions
- How to handle focus between panes (chat log vs input)
- Scroll state management approach
- How markdown rendering integrates with UiBlock
- Command history persistence (in-memory vs file)
- Theme system design
- How to surface errors to users (inline vs popup vs status bar)

1→use schemars::JsonSchema;
     2→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     3→use serde_json::Value;
     4→
     5→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
     6→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
     7→pub enum Role {
     8→    System,
     9→    User,
    10→    Assistant,
    11→    Tool,
    12→}
    13→
    14→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
    15→pub struct ChatMessage {
    16→    pub role: Role,
    17→    pub content: String,
    18→    /// For tool result messages, the ID of the tool call this responds to.
    19→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    20→    pub tool_call_id: Option<String>,
    21→}
    22→
    23→impl ChatMessage {
    24→    pub fn system(content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    25→        Self {
    26→            role: Role::System,
    27→            content: content.into(),
    28→            tool_call_id: None,
    29→        }
    30→    }
    31→
    32→    pub fn user(content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    33→        Self {
    34→            role: Role::User,
    35→            content: content.into(),
    36→            tool_call_id: None,
    37→        }
    38→    }
    39→
    40→    pub fn assistant(content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    41→        Self {
    42→            role: Role::Assistant,
    43→            content: content.into(),
    44→            tool_call_id: None,
    45→        }
    46→    }
    47→
    48→    pub fn tool(content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    49→        Self {
    50→            role: Role::Tool,
    51→            content: content.into(),
    52→            tool_call_id: None,
    53→        }
    54→    }
    55→
    56→    pub fn tool_result(call_id: impl Into<String>, content: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    57→        Self {
    58→            role: Role::Tool,
    59→            content: content.into(),
    60→            tool_call_id: Some(call_id.into()),
    61→        }
    62→    }
    63→}
    64→
    65→/// MCP-compatible behavioral annotations for tools.
    66→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
    67→pub struct ToolAnnotations {
    68→    /// Tool does not modify its environment.
    69→    #[serde(default)]
    70→    pub read_only: bool,
    71→    /// Tool may perform destructive updates.
    72→    #[serde(default)]
    73→    pub destructive: bool,
    74→    /// Repeated calls with same args produce same result.
    75→    #[serde(default)]
    76→    pub idempotent: bool,
    77→    /// Tool interacts with external entities (network, APIs).
    78→    #[serde(default)]
    79→    pub open_world: bool,
    80→    /// Tool requires user confirmation before execution.
    81→    #[serde(default)]
    82→    pub requires_confirmation: bool,
    83→}
    84→
    85→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
    86→pub struct ToolDefinition {
    87→    pub name: String,
    88→    pub description: String,
    89→    pub input_schema: Value,
    90→
    91→    // ── MCP-aligned fields (all optional, backward-compatible) ──
    92→    /// Human-readable display name (MCP: title).
    93→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    94→    pub title: Option<String>,
    95→    /// JSON Schema for structured output (MCP: outputSchema).
    96→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    97→    pub output_schema: Option<Value>,
    98→    /// Behavioral hints (MCP: annotations).
    99→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   100→    pub annotations: Option<ToolAnnotations>,
   101→
   102→    // ── Arcan extensions ──
   103→    /// Tool category for grouping ("filesystem", "code", "shell", "mcp").
   104→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   105→    pub category: Option<String>,
   106→    /// Tags for filtering and matching (skills.sh compatible).
   107→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty")]
   108→    pub tags: Vec<String>,
   109→    /// Maximum execution timeout in seconds.
   110→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   111→    pub timeout_secs: Option<u32>,
   112→}
   113→
   114→/// Typed content block in a tool result (MCP-compatible).
   115→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   116→#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   117→pub enum ToolContent {
   118→    Text { text: String },
   119→    Image { data: String, mime_type: String },
   120→    Json { value: Value },
   121→}
   122→
   123→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   124→pub struct ToolCall {
   125→    pub call_id: String,
   126→    pub tool_name: String,
   127→    #[serde(default)]
   128→    pub input: Value,
   129→}
   130→
   131→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   132→pub struct ToolResult {
   133→    pub call_id: String,
   134→    pub tool_name: String,
   135→    #[serde(default)]
   136→    pub output: Value,
   137→    /// MCP-style typed content blocks (optional, alongside output for compat).
   138→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   139→    pub content: Option<Vec<ToolContent>>,
   140→    /// Whether this result represents an error (MCP: isError).
   141→    #[serde(default)]
   142→    pub is_error: bool,
   143→    pub state_patch: Option<StatePatch>,
   144→}
   145→
   146→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   147→pub struct ToolResultSummary {
   148→    pub call_id: String,
   149→    pub tool_name: String,
   150→    #[serde(default)]
   151→    pub output: Value,
   152→}
   153→
   154→impl From<&ToolResult> for ToolResultSummary {
   155→    fn from(value: &ToolResult) -> Self {
   156→        Self {
   157→            call_id: value.call_id.clone(),
   158→            tool_name: value.tool_name.clone(),
   159→            output: value.output.clone(),
   160→        }
   161→    }
   162→}
   163→
   164→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
   165→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   166→pub enum StatePatchFormat {
   167→    JsonPatch,
   168→    MergePatch,
   169→}
   170→
   171→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
   172→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   173→pub enum StatePatchSource {
   174→    Model,
   175→    Tool,
   176→    System,
   177→}
   178→
   179→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   180→pub struct StatePatch {
   181→    pub format: StatePatchFormat,
   182→    #[serde(default)]
   183→    pub patch: Value,
   184→    pub source: StatePatchSource,
   185→}
   186→
   187→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
   188→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   189→pub enum ModelStopReason {
   190→    EndTurn,
   191→    ToolUse,
   192→    NeedsUser,
   193→    MaxTokens,
   194→    Safety,
   195→    Unknown,
   196→}
   197→
   198→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   199→#[serde(tag = "kind", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   200→pub enum ModelDirective {
   201→    Text { delta: String },
   202→    ToolCall { call: ToolCall },
   203→    StatePatch { patch: StatePatch },
   204→    FinalAnswer { text: String },
   205→}
   206→
   207→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   208→pub struct ModelTurn {
   209→    pub directives: Vec<ModelDirective>,
   210→    pub stop_reason: ModelStopReason,
   211→    /// Token usage for this turn (if reported by the provider).
   212→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   213→    pub usage: Option<TokenUsage>,
   214→}
   215→
   216→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
   217→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   218→pub struct TokenUsage {
   219→    /// Tokens in the input/prompt.
   220→    #[serde(default)]
   221→    pub input_tokens: u64,
   222→    /// Tokens in the output/completion.
   223→    #[serde(default)]
   224→    pub output_tokens: u64,
   225→    /// Tokens from cache reads (Anthropic-specific).
   226→    #[serde(default)]
   227→    pub cache_read_tokens: u64,
   228→    /// Tokens written to cache (Anthropic-specific).
   229→    #[serde(default)]
   230→    pub cache_creation_tokens: u64,
   231→}
   232→
   233→impl TokenUsage {
   234→    /// Accumulate another usage into this one.
   235→    pub fn accumulate(&mut self, other: &TokenUsage) {
   236→        self.input_tokens += other.input_tokens;
   237→        self.output_tokens += other.output_tokens;
   238→        self.cache_read_tokens += other.cache_read_tokens;
   239→        self.cache_creation_tokens += other.cache_creation_tokens;
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    pub fn total(&self) -> u64 {
   243→        self.input_tokens + self.output_tokens
   244→    }
   245→}
   246→
   247→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq, Eq)]
   248→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   249→pub enum RunStopReason {
   250→    Completed,
   251→    NeedsUser,
   252→    BlockedByPolicy,
   253→    BudgetExceeded,
   254→    Cancelled,
   255→    Error,
   256→}
   257→
   258→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, JsonSchema, PartialEq)]
   259→#[serde(tag = "part_type", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   260→pub enum AgentEvent {
   261→    RunStarted {
   262→        run_id: String,
   263→        session_id: String,
   264→        provider: String,
   265→        max_iterations: u32,
   266→    },
   267→    IterationStarted {
   268→        run_id: String,
   269→        session_id: String,
   270→        iteration: u32,
   271→    },
   272→    ModelOutput {
   273→        run_id: String,
   274→        session_id: String,
   275→        iteration: u32,
   276→        stop_reason: ModelStopReason,
   277→        directive_count: usize,
   278→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   279→        usage: Option<TokenUsage>,
   280→    },
   281→    TextDelta {
   282→        run_id: String,
   283→        session_id: String,
   284→        iteration: u32,
   285→        delta: String,
   286→    },
   287→    ToolCallRequested {
   288→        run_id: String,
   289→        session_id: String,
   290→        iteration: u32,
   291→        call: ToolCall,
   292→    },
   293→    ToolCallCompleted {
   294→        run_id: String,
   295→        session_id: String,
   296→        iteration: u32,
   297→        result: ToolResultSummary,
   298→    },
   299→    ToolCallFailed {
   300→        run_id: String,
   301→        session_id: String,
   302→        iteration: u32,
   303→        call_id: String,
   304→        tool_name: String,
   305→        error: String,
   306→    },
   307→    StatePatched {
   308→        run_id: String,
   309→        session_id: String,
   310→        iteration: u32,
   311→        patch: StatePatch,
   312→        revision: u64,
   313→    },
   314→    ContextCompacted {
   315→        run_id: String,
   316→        session_id: String,
   317→        iteration: u32,
   318→        dropped_count: usize,
   319→        tokens_before: usize,
   320→        tokens_after: usize,
   321→    },
   322→    ApprovalRequested {
   323→        run_id: String,
   324→        session_id: String,
   325→        approval_id: String,
   326→        call_id: String,
   327→        tool_name: String,
   328→        arguments: serde_json::Value,
   329→        /// Risk level as string ("low"/"medium"/"high"/"critical") to keep arcan-core lago-free.
   330→        risk: String,
   331→    },
   332→    ApprovalResolved {
   333→        run_id: String,
   334→        session_id: String,
   335→        approval_id: String,
   336→        /// Decision as string ("approved"/"denied"/"timeout").
   337→        decision: String,
   338→        reason: Option<String>,
   339→    },
   340→    RunErrored {
   341→        run_id: String,
   342→        session_id: String,
   343→        error: String,
   344→    },
   345→    RunFinished {
   346→        run_id: String,
   347→        session_id: String,
   348→        reason: RunStopReason,
   349→        total_iterations: u32,
   350→        final_answer: Option<String>,
   351→    },
   352→}
   353→
   354→impl AgentEvent {
   355→    pub fn as_sse_data(&self) -> Result<String, serde_json::Error> {
   356→        let payload = serde_json::to_string(self)?;
   357→        Ok(format!("data: {payload}\n\n"))
   358→    }
   359→}
   360→

You are a Rust software architect designing a comprehensive TUI restructure for the Arcan agent runtime. Design a detailed implementation plan based on these requirements and constraints.

## Current State
- arcan-tui crate: 5 source files, ~1,488 LOC
- Stack: ratatui 0.30, crossterm 0.29, tokio, reqwest, reqwest-eventsource
- Files: lib.rs (entry), app.rs (event loop), models.rs (state), network.rs (HTTP/SSE), ui.rs (rendering)
- 15 unit tests across 3 files, 0 integration tests
- Working features: basic chat, SSE streaming, tool execution display, approval workflow
- Major gaps: no scroll, no markdown, no input history, no timestamps, no status bar, stubbed model switching

## User Requirements
1. **Primary goal**: Usability first — scroll, input history, status bar, markdown, error visibility
2. **Dependencies**: Rich — add tui-textarea 0.7, tui-markdown, syntect-tui 3.0
3. **Refactoring**: Full restructure — redesign module layout, widget abstraction layer, component-based architecture

## Available Daemon API (currently unused by TUI)
- GET /sessions — list sessions
- POST /sessions — create session
- GET /sessions/{id}/state — agent state vector, operating mode
- GET /sessions/{id}/events — event history (paginated)
- GET /sessions/{id}/branches — list branches
- POST /sessions/{id}/branches — create branch
- POST /sessions/{id}/branches/{id}/merge — merge branch

## Available External Crates
- tui-textarea 0.7: Multi-line input, undo/redo, Emacs keybindings, selection, works with ratatui 0.30
- tui-markdown: Converts markdown to ratatui::Text via pulldown-cmark (experimental but functional)
- syntect-tui 3.0: Code syntax highlighting for ratatui

## Design Constraints
- Rust 2024 Edition (edition = "2024", rust-version = "1.85")
- `gen` is reserved keyword — never use as identifier
- Error handling: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for binaries
- Must maintain all 15 existing tests passing
- Pure state model (AppState) must remain IO-free and testable
- Network layer already supports 3 SSE formats (protocol, Vercel v6, canonical)

## Design the following:

### 1. New Module Architecture
Propose a complete file/module tree with clear responsibilities. Consider:
- Widget abstraction layer (trait or enum-based)
- Separate files for each UI panel/widget
- Focus management system
- Theme/style configuration
- Event routing

### 2. Implementation Phases
Break into 4-5 phases, each independently shippable:
- Phase 1: Foundation (restructure + scroll + status bar)
- Phase 2: Rich input (tui-textarea + command history)
- Phase 3: Markdown + syntax highlighting
- Phase 4: Polish (error visibility, timestamps, tool results, loading indicators)
- Phase 5: Advanced features (session browser, state inspector — stretch)

### 3. For each phase, specify:
- Files to create/modify
- Key structs/traits/functions
- Dependencies added
- Tests to add
- Verification steps

### 4. Key Design Decisions
- How to handle focus between panes (chat log vs input)
- Scroll state management approach
- How markdown rendering integrates with UiBlock
- Command history persistence (in-memory vs file)
- Theme system design
- How to surface errors to users (inline vs popup vs status bar)

Provide a concrete, actionable plan with file paths relative to arcan/crates/arcan-tui/.

content.content:
200→pub fn openapi_spec() -> utoipa::openapi::OpenApi {
   201→    ApiDoc::openapi()
   202→}
   203→
   204→async fn openapi_json() -> Json<utoipa::openapi::OpenApi> {
   205→    Json(ApiDoc::openapi())
   206→}
   207→
   208→// ─── Router ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   209→
   210→pub fn create_canonical_router(runtime: Arc<KernelRuntime>) -> Router {
   211→    let state = CanonicalState { runtime };
   212→    Router::new()
   213→        .route("/health", get(health))
   214→        .route("/openapi.json", get(openapi_json))
   215→        .route("/sessions", get(list_sessions).post(create_session))
   216→        .route("/sessions/{session_id}/runs", post(run_session))
   217→        .route("/sessions/{session_id}/state", get(get_state))
   218→        .route("/sessions/{session_id}/events", get(list_events))
   219→        .route("/sessions/{session_id}/events/stream", get(stream_events))
   220→        .route(
   221→            "/sessions/{session_id}/branches",
   222→            post(create_branch).get(list_branches),
   223→        )
   224→        .route(
   225→            "/sessions/{session_id}/branches/{branch_id}/merge",
   226→            post(merge_branch),
   227→        )
   228→        .route(
   229→            "/sessions/{session_id}/approvals/{approval_id}",
   230→            post(resolve_approval),
   231→        )
   232→        .with_state(state)
   233→}
   234→
   235→// ─── Stream format ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   236→
   237→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
   238→enum StreamFormat {
   239→    Canonical,
   240→    VercelAiSdkV6,
   241→}
   242→
   243→impl StreamFormat {
   244→    fn from_query(value: Option<&str>) -> Self {
   245→        match value {
   246→            Some("vercel_ai_sdk_v6" | "aisdk_v6") => Self::VercelAiSdkV6,
   247→            _ => Self::Canonical,
   248→        }
   249→    }
   250→}
   251→
   252→// ─── Handlers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   253→
   254→#[utoipa::path(
   255→    get,
   256→    path = "/health",
   257→    tag = "health",
   258→    responses(
   259→        (status = 200, description = "Daemon is healthy", body = Object)
   260→    )
   261→)]
   262→async fn health() -> Json<serde_json::Value> {
   263→    Json(json!({ "status": "ok" }))
   264→}
   265→
   266→#[utoipa::path(
   267→    get,
   268→    path = "/sessions",
   269→    tag = "sessions",
   270→    responses(
   271→        (status = 200, description = "List of sessions", body = Vec<SessionSummary>)
   272→    )
   273→)]
   274→async fn list_sessions(State(state): State<CanonicalState>) -> Json<Vec<SessionSummary>> {
   275→    let manifests = state.runtime.list_sessions();
   276→    let mut summaries: Vec<SessionSummary> = manifests
   277→        .into_iter()
   278→        .map(|m| SessionSummary {
   279→            session_id: m.session_id.as_str().to_owned(),
   280→            owner: m.owner,
   281→            created_at: m.created_at,
   282→        })
   283→        .collect();
   284→    summaries.sort_by(|a, b| b.created_at.cmp(&a.created_at));
   285→    Json(summaries)
   286→}
   287→
   288→async fn persist_last_session_hint(runtime: &KernelRuntime, session_id: &SessionId) {
   289→    let path = runtime.root_path().join("last_session");
   290→    let _ = fs::write(path, session_id.as_str()).await;
   291→}
   292→
   293→#[utoipa::path(
   294→    post,

data.prompt:
You are a Rust software architect designing a comprehensive TUI restructure for the Arcan agent runtime. Design a detailed implementation plan based on these requirements and constraints.

## Current State
- arcan-tui crate: 5 source files, ~1,488 LOC
- Stack: ratatui 0.30, crossterm 0.29, tokio, reqwest, reqwest-eventsource
- Files: lib.rs (entry), app.rs (event loop), models.rs (state), network.rs (HTTP/SSE), ui.rs (rendering)
- 15 unit tests across 3 files, 0 integration tests
- Working features: basic chat, SSE streaming, tool execution display, approval workflow
- Major gaps: no scroll, no markdown, no input history, no timestamps, no status bar, stubbed model switching

## User Requirements
1. **Primary goal**: Usability first — scroll, input history, status bar, markdown, error visibility
2. **Dependencies**: Rich — add tui-textarea 0.7, tui-markdown, syntect-tui 3.0
3. **Refactoring**: Full restructure — redesign module layout, widget abstraction layer, component-based architecture

## Available Daemon API (currently unused by TUI)
- GET /sessions — list sessions
- POST /sessions — create session
- GET /sessions/{id}/state — agent state vector, operating mode
- GET /sessions/{id}/events — event history (paginated)
- GET /sessions/{id}/branches — list branches
- POST /sessions/{id}/branches — create branch
- POST /sessions/{id}/branches/{id}/merge — merge branch

## Available External Crates
- tui-textarea 0.7: Multi-line input, undo/redo, Emacs keybindings, selection, works with ratatui 0.30
- tui-markdown: Converts markdown to ratatui::Text via pulldown-cmark (experimental but functional)
- syntect-tui 3.0: Code syntax highlighting for ratatui

## Design Constraints
- Rust 2024 Edition (edition = "2024", rust-version = "1.85")
- `gen` is reserved keyword — never use as identifier
- Error handling: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for binaries
- Must maintain all 15 existing tests passing
- Pure state model (AppState) must remain IO-free and testable
- Network layer already supports 3 SSE formats (protocol, Vercel v6, canonical)

## Design the following:

### 1. New Module Architecture
Propose a complete file/module tree with clear responsibilities. Consider:
- Widget abstraction layer (trait or enum-based)
- Separate files for each UI panel/widget
- Focus management system
- Theme/style configuration
- Event routing

### 2. Implementation Phases
Break into 4-5 phases, each independently shippable:
- Phase 1: Foundation (restructure + scroll + status bar)
- Phase 2: Rich input (tui-textarea + command history)
- Phase 3: Markdown + syntax highlighting
- Phase 4: Polish (error visibility, timestamps, tool results, loading indicators)
- Phase 5: Advanced features (session browser, state inspector — stretch)

### 3. For each phase, specify:
- Files to create/modify
- Key structs/traits/functions
- Dependencies added
- Tests to add
- Verification steps

### 4. Key Design Decisions
- How to handle focus between panes (chat log vs input)
- Scroll state management approach
- How markdown rendering integrates with UiBlock
- Command history persistence (in-memory vs file)
- Theme system design
- How to surface errors to users (inline vs popup vs status bar)

Provide a concrete, actionable plan with file paths relative to arcan/crates/arcan-tui/.

   122→

   130→

   163→        create_session,
   164→        run_session,
   165→        get_state,
   166→        list_events,
   167→        stream_events,
   168→        create_branch,
   169→        list_branches,
   170→        merge_branch,
   171→        resolve_approval,
   172→    ),
   173→    components(schemas(
   174→        CreateSessionRequest,
   175→        RunRequest,
   176→        ProposedToolRequest,
   177→        RunResponse,
   178→        StateResponse,
   179→        EventListResponse,
   180→        BranchListResponse,
   181→        BranchMergeResponse,
   182→        SessionSummary,
   183→        CreateBranchRequest,
   184→        MergeBranchRequest,
   185→        ResolveApprovalRequest,
   186→        ErrorResponse,
   187→    )),

   190→        (name = "sessions", description = "Session lifecycle"),
   191→        (name = "runs", description = "Agent run execution"),
   192→        (name = "events", description = "Event log and streaming"),
   193→        (name = "branches", description = "Branch management"),
   194→        (name = "approvals", description = "Approval workflow"),
   195→    )
   196→)]

You are a Rust software architect designing a comprehensive TUI restructure for the Arcan agent runtime. Design a detailed implementation plan based on these requirements and constraints.

## Current State
- arcan-tui crate: 5 source files, ~1,488 LOC
- Stack: ratatui 0.30, crossterm 0.29, tokio, reqwest, reqwest-eventsource

- 15 unit tests across 3 files, 0 integration tests

- Major gaps: no scroll, no markdown, no input history, no timestamps, no status bar, stubbed model switching

1. **Primary goal**: Usability first — scroll, input history, status bar, markdown, error visibility

- POST /sessions — create session

- GET /sessions/{id}/events — event history (paginated)
- GET /sessions/{id}/branches — list branches

- tui-textarea 0.7: Multi-line input, undo/redo, Emacs keybindings, selection, works with ratatui 0.30

## Design Constraints
- Rust 2024 Edition (edition = "2024", rust-version = "1.85")
- `gen` is reserved keyword — never use as identifier
- Error handling: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for binaries
- Must maintain all 15 existing tests passing

- Network layer already supports 3 SSE formats (protocol, Vercel v6, canonical)

- Event routing

- Phase 4: Polish (error visibility, timestamps, tool results, loading indicators)
- Phase 5: Advanced features (session browser, state inspector — stretch)

You are a Rust software architect designing a comprehensive TUI restructure for the Arcan agent runtime. Design a detailed implementation plan based on these requirements and constraints.

## Current State
- arcan-tui crate: 5 source files, ~1,488 LOC
- Stack: ratatui 0.30, crossterm 0.29, tokio, reqwest, reqwest-eventsource
- Files: lib.rs (entry), app.rs (event loop), models.rs (state), network.rs (HTTP/SSE), ui.rs (rendering)
- 15 unit tests across 3 files, 0 integration tests
- Working features: basic chat, SSE streaming, tool execution display, approval workflow
- Major gaps: no scroll, no markdown, no input history, no timestamps, no status bar, stubbed model switching

## User Requirements
1. **Primary goal**: Usability first — scroll, input history, status bar, markdown, error visibility
2. **Dependencies**: Rich — add tui-textarea 0.7, tui-markdown, syntect-tui 3.0
3. **Refactoring**: Full restructure — redesign module layout, widget abstraction layer, component-based architecture

## Available Daemon API (currently unused by TUI)
- GET /sessions — list sessions
- POST /sessions — create session
- GET /sessions/{id}/state — agent state vector, operating mode
- GET /sessions/{id}/events — event history (paginated)
- GET /sessions/{id}/branches — list branches
- POST /sessions/{id}/branches — create branch
- POST /sessions/{id}/branches/{id}/merge — merge branch

## Available External Crates
- tui-textarea 0.7: Multi-line input, undo/redo, Emacs keybindings, selection, works with ratatui 0.30
- tui-markdown: Converts markdown to ratatui::Text via pulldown-cmark (experimental but functional)
- syntect-tui 3.0: Code syntax highlighting for ratatui

## Design Constraints
- Rust 2024 Edition (edition = "2024", rust-version = "1.85")
- `gen` is reserved keyword — never use as identifier
- Error handling: thiserror for libraries, anyhow for binaries
- Must maintain all 15 existing tests passing
- Pure state model (AppState) must remain IO-free and testable
- Network layer already supports 3 SSE formats (protocol, Vercel v6, canonical)

## Design the following:

### 1. New Module Architecture
Propose a complete file/module tree with clear responsibilities. Consider:
- Widget abstraction layer (trait or enum-based)
- Separate files for each UI panel/widget
- Focus management system
- Theme/style configuration
- Event routing

### 2. Implementation Phases
Break into 4-5 phases, each independently shippable:
- Phase 1: Foundation (restructure + scroll + status bar)
- Phase 2: Rich input (tui-textarea + command history)
- Phase 3: Markdown + syntax highlighting
- Phase 4: Polish (error visibility, timestamps, tool results, loading indicators)
- Phase 5: Advanced features (session browser, state inspector — stretch)

### 3. For each phase, specify:
- Files to create/modify
- Key structs/traits/functions
- Dependencies added
- Tests to add
- Verification steps

### 4. Key Design Decisions
- How to handle focus between panes (chat log vs input)
- Scroll state management approach
- How markdown rendering integrates with UiBlock
- Command history persistence (in-memory vs file)
- Theme system design
- How to surface errors to users (inline vs popup vs status bar)

Provide a concrete, actionable plan with file paths relative to arcan/crates/arcan-tui/.

Links: [{"title":"tui_markdown - Rust","url":"https://docs.rs/tui-markdown"},{"title":"tui-markdown 0.3.7 - Docs.rs","url":"https://docs.rs/crate/tui-markdown/latest"},{"title":"tui-markdown - crates.io: Rust Package Registry","url":"https://crates.io/crates/tui-markdown"},{"title":"Tui-markdown — CLI for Rust // Lib.rs","url":"https://lib.rs/crates/tui-markdown"},{"title":"pulldown_cmark - Rust","url":"https://docs.rs/pulldown-cmark/latest/pulldown_cmark/"},{"title":"pulldown-cmark - crates.io: Rust Package Registry","url":"https://crates.io/crates/pulldown-cmark"},{"title":"pulldown-cmark — Rust utility // Lib.rs","url":"https://lib.rs/crates/pulldown-cmark"},{"title":"GitHub - joshka/tui-markdown","url":"https://github.com/joshka/tui-markdown"},{"title":"markdown-reader — Rust application // Lib.rs","url":"https://lib.rs/crates/markdown-reader"},{"title":"GitHub - ratatui/ratatui: A Rust crate for cooking up terminal user interfaces (TUIs) 👨‍🍳🐀 https://ratatui.rs","url":"https://github.com/ratatui/ratatui"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about tui-markdown, ratatui, and pulldown-cmark:

## tui-markdown

tui-markdown is a simple markdown renderer widget for Ratatui that uses the pulldown-cmark crate to parse markdown and convert it to a Text widget. It's an experimental Proof of Concept library for converting markdown content to a Ratatui Text value.

The latest version appears to be 0.3.7, released on December 27, 2025. The crate depends on pulldown-cmark ^0.13.0, along with other dependencies like ratatui-core and optional syntax highlighting support via syntect.

## pulldown-cmark

pulldown-cmark provides a Parser struct which is an iterator over Events that can be used directly or to output HTML using the HTML module. It uses pull parsing, which has become popular especially for memory-conscious applications because it uses dramatically less memory than constructing a document tree.

## Ratatui

Ratatui is a Rust crate for cooking up terminal user interfaces (TUIs) that provides a simple and flexible way to create text-based user interfaces in the terminal.

These three components work together to enable markdown rendering in terminal-based user interfaces built with Ratatui.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's information about the **tui-textarea 0.7 Rust crate** and its API features:

## Overview

tui-textarea is a simple yet powerful text editor widget for ratatui and tui-rs. Multi-line text editor can be easily put as part of your TUI application.

## Key API Features

The crate includes yank support and paste text deleted with C-k, C-j. It is backend agnostic, with crossterm, termion, termwiz, and custom backends all supported.

### Configuration & Customization

- The default tab width is 4, and can be changed using TextArea::set_tab_length() method.
- By default, past 50 modifications are stored as edit history which is used for undo/redo, and this can be changed using TextArea::set_max_histories() method.

### Text Search

If you need text search with regular expressions, enable the search feature, which adds regex crate as dependency.

### Multiple Backend Support

Crossterm backend support is enabled by default, and for ratatui with termion or termwiz, you enable the termion or termwiz feature instead of crossterm feature.

The crate maintains compatibility with both ratatui and tui-rs frameworks with appropriate feature flags and configuration options.

Links: [{"title":"syntect-tui — CLI for Rust // Lib.rs","url":"https://lib.rs/crates/syntect-tui"},{"title":"tui_syntax_highlight - Rust","url":"https://docs.rs/tui-syntax-highlight"},{"title":"feat(tui): syntax highlighting via syntect with theme picker by fcoury · Pull Request #11447 · openai/codex","url":"https://github.com/openai/codex/pull/11447"},{"title":"GitHub - chanq-io/syntect-tui: Lightweight translation layer between syntect.rs and ratatui.rs style types","url":"https://github.com/chanq-io/syntect-tui"},{"title":"GitHub - aschey/tui-syntax-highlight: A syntax highlighter for Ratatui apps","url":"https://github.com/aschey/tui-syntax-highlight"},{"title":"GitHub - ratatui/awesome-ratatui: A curated list of TUI apps and libraries built with Ratatui","url":"https://github.com/ratatui/awesome-ratatui"},{"title":"tui_markdown - Rust","url":"https://docs.rs/tui-markdown/latest/tui_markdown/"},{"title":"syntect-tui 3.0.6 - Docs.rs","url":"https://docs.rs/crate/syntect-tui/latest"},{"title":"syntect_tui - Rust","url":"https://docs.rs/syntect-tui/latest/syntect_tui/"},{"title":"GitHub - trishume/syntect: Rust library for syntax highlighting using Sublime Text syntax definitions.","url":"https://github.com/trishume/syntect"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about syntect-tui 3.0 and Ratatui syntax highlighting:

## Overview

syntect-tui is a lightweight translation layer between syntect and ratatui style types. If you're building a CLI app with a UI powered by Ratatui and need syntax highlighting, then you may find this crate useful.

## Version 3.0

You can install syntect-tui version 3.0 by adding `syntect-tui = "3.0"` to your Cargo.toml.

## How It Works

syntect-tui parses code, highlights it using syntect and converts it into ratatui::text::Line ready for rendering in a tui application. The crate provides functions like `into_span` that convert syntect highlighting segments into ratatui Span objects compatible with the framework.

## Project Status

Given the limited scope of this crate the maintainer does not have plans to extend existing functionality much further, however they are open to requests and/or contributions, so contributors are encouraged to fork and submit pull requests.

The crate is available on crates.io and actively maintained, with documentation available on both the GitHub repository and docs.rs.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.content:
# Main API Functions

The primary functions for rendering markdown are:

1. **`from_str`** — "Render Markdown `input` into a [`ratatui::text::Text`] using the default [`Options`]."

2. **`from_str_with_options`** — "Render Markdown `input` into a [`ratatui::text::Text`] using the supplied [`Options`]."

## Function Signatures

```rust
fn from_str(input: &str) -> Text
fn from_str_with_options(input: &str, options: Options) -> Text
```

Both functions accept a markdown string as input and return a `Text` widget that can be rendered using Ratatui. The second variant allows customization through an `Options` configuration struct.

content.content:
# tui_textarea Main Structures and Methods

## Primary Struct: TextArea

**TextArea** manages the editor state for a multi-line text input widget. It implements ratatui's `Widget` trait for rendering.

### Constructors

- `TextArea::default()` - Creates an empty textarea
- `TextArea::new(Vec<String>)` - Initializes with text lines
- `TextArea::from()` - Converts iterators of string-like types

### Key Methods

| Method | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `input(key)` | Processes keyboard events with default mappings |
| `input_without_shortcuts(input)` | Handles basic operations only |
| `lines()` | Returns `&[String]` reference to text |
| `into_lines()` | Consumes textarea, returns `Vec<String>` |
| `move_cursor(CursorMove)` | Repositions cursor |
| `set_line_number_style(Style)` | Enables/configures line numbers |
| `set_cursor_line_style(Style)` | Customizes cursor line appearance |
| `set_tab_length(usize)` | Adjusts tab width (default: 4) |
| `set_max_histories(usize)` | Controls undo/redo buffer |

### Supported Events

The widget handles approximately 50+ key combinations including:
- **Navigation**: Ctrl+F/B, Up/Down, word/paragraph movement
- **Editing**: Insert/delete characters, newlines, words, entire lines
- **Operations**: Undo/Redo, Copy/Cut/Paste, Text selection
- **Search**: Pattern matching with regex (requires `search` feature)
- **Scrolling**: Page Up/Down navigation
